entry.title: This week’s outside five sigma (#42)
entry.content:
Bioresorbable optoelectronic pacemaker

A grain of rice size pacemaker that is fully bioresorbable… (mostly made with Mg, Mo, and Si) It even got a phototransistor for wireless optoelectronic control.

  • they could put them on the valve replacement? Looks crazy.
  • Zhang2025: Millimetre-scale bioresorbable optoelectronic systems for electrotherapy
  • (also)
  • There is a separate system outside on the skip to go with them.
  • Its battery supplier PowerStream has a lovely website with “add to cart” buttons next every in-stock items

NeRF-based tomographic reconstruction

of course this has been done, makes sense

(using NeRF and 3DGS for x-ray tomography)

  • Theres quite a few. The above is from:
  • Zha2024: R2-Gaussian: Rectifying Radiative Gaussian Splatting for Tomographic Reconstruction

Collection of chips next to beans and nuts

  • First image from this tweet
  • Second image from Martinez2023: A chip-scale atomic beam clock

Digital mechanical lock with metamaterials

a digital mechanical door lock

  • Ion2017: Digital Mechanical Metamaterials
  • there is a video, looks pretty sick: Digital Mechanical Metamaterials
  • probably printed the channel too tight, takes quite some force & can’t really cascade because of the force would accumulate too fast & would not continue triggering without me pushing on the whole chain

Mechanical computing with metamaterials

this is so slow.. how about we do this at the crystal lattice level with THz clock rate

  • Byun2024: Integrated mechanical computing for autonomous soft machines
  • this and the digital mechanical door lock earlier today were found in this review, it is open access and has a lot of fancy mech stuff inside.
  • Feels like this kind of mech computing is really for weird applications in weird environments
  • Chen2025: Advances in metamaterials for mechanical computing

Diamond via for thermal management

I’ve seen copper via, tungsten via, superconducting via, via for light… diamond via for heat is a first time.

  • Malakoutian2025: Diamond thermal matrix demonstration for two-tier 3D integrated circuits
  • this is where the stock market is going on Monday
  • yes that

Twisted photonic crystal sensor

I don’t like “twistronics” but this twisted photonic crystal is pretty crazy

imagine bonding two chips with a sub um gap around the ~ 1 mm central area, and rotate one of them against the other one

  • Tang2025: An adaptive moiré sensor for spectro-polarimetric hyperimaging (also)

Visual data over analog telephone

Welcome to April 1925, today we are sending light (pictures) as electrical signals instead of the usual electrical signals as light (fiber optic telecom)

  • The Transmission of Pictures Over Telephone Lines

ML-based spectroscopy decoding

in some fields people would rather share code with a screenshot in a paper instead of a github repo, and they are proud of it

(and they should, most people don’t even talk about the code behind the work)

  • Vizoso2025: Decoding diffraction and spectroscopy data with machine learning: A tutorial

Charge-stabilized droplet does not splash

video

if you don’t want droplets to splash when hitting a surface, charge them up

  • video from: Charged Droplets Can Hit a Surface Without Splashing
  • it’s because the lamella gets attracted to the surface from electrostatic force.
  • Yu2025: Why Charged Drops Do Not Splash
  • it is charged by pass it thru a biased ring

Triboelectric droplet LED generator

video

powering LEDs with water droplets, not from the kinetic energy but from charge transfer

(they are using 400 LEDs because the optimal load resistance is ~ 1 MΩ, and peak power density is actually as high as 50 W/m^2, and efficiency of 2.2%, kind of crazy)

  • Xu2020: A droplet-based electricity generator with high instantaneous power density (also)

quantum circuits vs refuelling plan

  • left: Low-Depth Unitary Quantum Circuits for Dualities in One-Dimensional Quantum Lattice Models
  • right: Bob’s Tale of Phantoms

Gaussian Splatting SLAM

video

maybe i should have upgraded my PC. So many projects need CUDA.. (this is MASt3R-SLAM)

  • MASt3R-SLAM: Real-Time Dense SLAM with 3D Reconstruction Priors
  • WildGS-SLAM: Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM in Dynamic Environments
  • also

Real-time drift correction in microscopy

example open source status in many fields (video):

  • paper is paywalled
  • code is in MATLAB, and requires the parallel computing toolbox (??)

still very useful tho, this one is a real-time 3D drift correction for super-resolution microscopy.

  • video from: NanoDriftGuard
  • Sun2025: NanoDriftGuard: Open-source isotropic ångström-scale active stabilization for super-resolution microscopy

Photonic processors for AI acceleration

“Quad-core photonic processor” from Lightmatter

Their “friend” Lightelligence also put out a similar work at the same time

  • Universal photonic artificial intelligence acceleration (also)
  • A New Kind of Computer
  • An integrated large-scale photonic accelerator with ultralow latency

Optical component defect detection

Training YOLO to inspect optical components for you

  • Huang2025: Surface defect detection of planar optical components based on OPT-YOLO
  • “In order to promote the development of the research field of surface defects in optical components, this article discloses some defect datasets at”
  • JLU-OPT

Intercellular mitochondrial transfer

mitochondria can move between cells??

i had no idea organ donation could happen at the cellular level.

  • Borcherding2023: The power and potential of mitochondria transfer (also)

Magic Leap waveguide manufacturing

wow Magic Leap has their own waveguide fab (video)

  • Magic Leap - Manufacturing
  • Here are some good background read:
  • Meta Orion AR Glasses: The first DEEP DIVE into the optical architecture
  • and Karl Guttag’s blog: Meta Orion AR Glasses (Pt. 1 Waveguides)

Supercritical gas circuit breaker

“A high-voltage circuit breaker interrupter, like this one made by GE Vernova, stops current by mechanically creating a gap and an arc, and then blasting high-pressure gas through the gap.”

  • How High-voltage Circuit Breakers Work
  • From First Supercritical CO2 Circuit Breaker Debuts
  • how the whole thing looks like:
  • IEEE Switchgear Committee, 2017 - AC High-Voltage Circuit Breakers

Data center power density map

look at Virginia

  • International Energy Agency report - Energy and AI
  • havent thought about MWh per wafer before
  • datacenter capacities
  • they explained why Northern Virginia
  • VEDP - Data Centers

2D materials in integrated photonics

“I have a probe I could tap and scan, what should I do with it?”

“Yes.”

  • Roadmap for Photonics with 2D Materials

Queryable materials property database

video

I had this random thought: why can’t I curl or wget some material properties.

Turns out you almost can. This one is quite nice, and got built-in API. If you are confused about Brillouin Zone etc, it even got interactive plots to help you get even more confused.

it’s just not as comprehensive or fit your need as much, but you could contribute to the properties.

  • The Materials Project - LiNbO₃ - mp-3731
  • How is there no option for electro-optic and nonlinear optic properties..

Wire-driven mobile robot with grappling

video

this robot moves around with “drone grappling hooks”

  • Overcoming Physical Limitations Utilizing the Environment with a Wire-Driven Multipurpose Robot

GUI parsing with OmniParser

Trying out OmniParser on comsol gui and it froze my poor M4 mini.. so here is part of the gui

  • OmniParser: Screen Parsing tool for Pure Vision Based GUI Agent
  • it’s unlikely aware of the structure of the components. It returns a list of components and their properties, including text from OCR, and icons from YOLO, and tries to guess what the icons mean

Flexure mechanism in NIF fusion experiments

automatic repost of sexy flexure

  • Thompson2025: Identifying a key culprit behind inertial confinement fusion’s ‘drive deficit’ problem

Projects

Procrastinating:

  • Droplet walker
  • TabTreeTracker
  • Low cost robot arm
  • Joystick doomscrolling

Planning:

  • Wave demo with ~ 300 Hz water surface wave + 3D printed components

Ongoing:

  • PIC pytorch
    • week 3 (42): [redacted]
    • week 2 (41): [redacted]
    • week 1 (40): built a few simple components. Tested simple backprop and training.

    • week 2 (40): Started wearing the camera at home & send thru wifi to desktop. Need to somehow save or send the photo to my phone when outside.
    • week 1 (39): I finally connected to the camera. Quality bad. Ordered new camera (OV5640) from aliexpress.

    • Week 1 (38): Gave a talk at PsiQuantum. I like the hat.
  • interactive wave
    • week 1 (37): made a python standalone library, see BPM.
  • fiber2chip blog
  • backyard landscaping:
    • week 16 (42): continue cinder block wall, finished upper level. Had to cast another small chunk of concrete
    • week 15 (41): continue cinder block wall, added geogrid and two more layers of blocks. Also tear down stair form OSB
    • week 14 (40): no progress this week
    • week 13 (39): finished stairs. Started wall with cinder block. Formed and poured concrete at the turn
    • week 12 (38): traveled to LA this weekend! No backyard work.
    • week 11 (37): working on the form of the stairs, deck joists
    • week 10 (36): did not work on it
    • week 9 (35): finished the deck joists and boarded the side of the deck
    • week 8 (34): finished jacuzzi base & deck frame
    • week 7 (33): working on base for Jacuzzi
    • week 6 (32): new year
    • week 5 (31): flattened the bottom level, put down form for concrete curb. Put down linear / grass barrier, and then gravel
    • week 4 (30): Finished the bottom retaining wall
    • week 3 (29): concrete poured for retaining wall beams and deck posts, started putting on retaining wall panels
    • week 2 (28): dig holes, posts inside holes, worked on Living room
    • week 1 (27): first week

Finished:

  • (week 32:) SiPho writeup for avi’s zine.
    • week 36: published on blog
  • Continuing [redacted]
    • Week 36: This has been finished.

Gave up

  • Traveling wave excitation of mechanical ring resonator (see this thread)
  • Tiny Tapeout
    • abort? (because of efabless no funding)

entry.title: This week’s outside five sigma (#41)
entry.content:
Designing for Century-Scale Accelerators

Imagine the thing you are designing won’t be in use until 60 years later

  • The biggest machine in science: inside the fight to build the next giant particle collider

Air-Gap Qubit with Niobium Bridges

they are so confident in their niobium air bridges process, that they actually made a transmon qubit with a vacuum gap capacitor with it, and it’s indeed really good.

  • Bruckmoser2025: Niobium Air Bridges as a Low-Loss Component for Superconducting Quantum Hardware

Nanostructured Alloys under TEM

there are tiny cathedrals everywhere for those with TEMs to see

  • Hornbuckle2025: A high-temperature nanostructured Cu-Ta-Li alloy with complexion-stabilized precipitates
  • i found you don’t need subscription to access the figures if you know the address lol
  • Fig. 1
  • Fig. 2

Neural Network-Based Photonics Design

You are using Synopsys OptSim/Ansys Lumerical INTERCONNECT/Luceda IPKISS/GDSFactory+ for your photonics circuit simulation, I’m using pytorch, we are not the same.

(this is probably a dumb idea but hey it got built-in tolerance analysis called backpropagation, and many benchmarking, visualization stuff)

(Will make the coupler modeling more accurate & add more components etc)

  • this should be freaking illegal man, how is getting a tolerance analysis this easy
  • now with non-ideal directional coupler
  • playing with optimization… This thing is SO EASY to run, here is before and after optimizing for 45:55 splitting for a wide bandwidth.
  • I’m going to do all my future fitting/optimization in pytorch. Everything is a nn for me now.
  • (of course this is purely for fun, it’s not easy to shape coupling in frequency domain, but will think about how to do this along direction of propagation which is much more practical)
  • optimizing just shifting and scaling of detuning and coupling (more practical than arbitrary detuning and coupling), and doing it over five segments
  • Quite some improvements between before and after.
  • implemented a ring resonator and added to one arm of the mzi
  • hopefully will find time tmw to test optimization for the linearity of the mzi slope like in this work
  • Got the optimization running, it’s tricky and converges to weird local min all the time.
  • Here’s a decent one. The ring is so low-Q it’s pretty much a bad MZI.
  • Now I think about it, you could always cascade MZIs with harmonic frequency to compose whatever response you want like a

Passive Millimeter-Wave Imaging Concepts

oh man the future is going to be beautiful, everyone will have their own handheld passive millimeter-wave camera with amazing rf and photonics/optics inside

  • Prather2025: The Role of Photonic Devices in Advanced Phased Array Antennas
  • now after thinking about this a bit, why not a big slab of vapor cell, and optically control & readout Rydberg atoms?
  • this might get rid of the expensive mmw LNAs, maybe the responsivity does not work out at all. Time to figure out more numbers
  • Legaie2023: A millimeter-wave atomic receiver

Rolled-Up RF Nanomembrane Resonator

Big LC resonator taking up too much area on your chip? Roll it up

  • Wei2025: On-chip compact radio frequency metal self-rolled-up nanomembrane resonator

Rubin Observatory’s Gigapixel Camera

look at this 3.2 gigapixel camera I just found, it only weights about three tons.

  • Explore - How Rubin Works - Rubin Technology - Camera
  • Roodman2024: LSST camera verification testing and characterization
  • 201 CCDs, each with 4k x 4k pixels, focal plane is 64cm x 64cm
  • look at this goofy camera
  • LSST Camera Installation
  • Rubin Technology
  • hell yeah please spend my tax money on larger cameras
  • we might be able to crowd fund this with 400,000 people (it was ~$168 million)
  • SLAC Completes Largest Digital Camera Ever Built for Astronomy

High-Speed Laser Additive Manufacturing

i want this to fit on my desk

(powder-based laser material deposition, titanium alloy Ti-6Al-4V, using a top-hat beam profile to increase the rate to 5 kg/h)

  • Saleh2025: Laser additive manufacturing of TI-6AL-4V alloy at a high deposition-rate using a rectangular laser spot
  • sorry I should have mentioned the ref was in the comment, here’s the section about their laser

Wafer-Level Sealed Vapor Cells

Make seal and break seal with a laser beam for wafer-level millimeter-scale vapor cells (single-use though)

  • Maurice2022: Wafer-level vapor cells filled with laser-actuated hermetic seals for integrated atomic devices
  • whenever there’s wafer bonding involved it’s pretty annoying

3D Coil-Based Full-Motion Haptics

This haptic actuator uses a three-axis tri-coil to push a magnet around on your skin.

They call them full freedom-of-motion actuators, could generate stroking, pinching/stretching, tapping etc., and they showed hand navigation with one such actuator, and virtual texture sensation (shear <-> friction coefficient, vibration frequency <-> spatial frequency of surface roughness etc.)

  • these coils look clean. A lot of details in the SI.
  • Ha2025: Full freedom-of-motion actuators as advanced haptic interfaces
  • fig1
  • fig2
  • fig3

Optical Fiber I/O for AI Systems

automatic repost of exponential curves of compute and communication

(the paper is about a 1.6 Tbps high-density I/O with 2D optical fiber array from Nubis Communications. Feels so wrong seeing optical fibers terminated with LGA package)

  • Valicourt2025: 1.6-Tbps low-power linear-drive high-density optical interface for machine learning/artificial intelligence

Traveling-Wave MEMS Micromotor

It’s kind of funny and counterintuitive that when you move big things with a traveling acoustic/mechanical wave, the direction they move would be the opposite direction of the wave.

(this micromotor is driven by a traveling wave)

(the last gif is a cross-section view, if you follow the displacement arrows near the surface, you can see why things get moved to the opposite direction)

(when things are smaller than the acoustic wavelength it would be different)

  • Xie2025: A MEMS traveling-wave micromotor-based miniature gyrocompass

Discovery of New Surface EM Modes

just ran into a new type of surface em wave that I have never heard about before and now I can’t sleep please send help

  • this one is more plasmonic ish. the one above works for all frequencies/bands at the interface, and one of the materials need to be anisotropic

Ultrasonic Focusing via mechanical metamaterial

This is how you call it, “a transmission mask with spatially designed holes”, instead of [redacted]

  • Hasegawa2025: Focusing of airborne ultrasound emitted by a flexurally vibrating plate using a transmission mask with spatially designed holes

Kinetic Inductance Detectors for Imaging

one alternative is cryogenic kinetic inductance detector array, also way more sensitive

  • Sayers2025: A millimeter-wave photometric camera for long-range imaging through optical obscurants using kinetic inductance detectors
  • McCarrick2018: Design and performance of dual-polarization lumped-element kinetic inductance detectors for millimeter-wave polarimetrylumped-element kinetic inductance detectors for
  • they are superconducting rf/microwave resonators that you could multiplex the readout in frequency domain, and their resonance frequencies shift when they are hit with mmw/optical photons, from the kinetic inductance increase when the photons break cooper pairs.

On-Board and Co-Packaged Optics at OFC 2025

so many on-board optics and co-packaged optics (last pic is co-packaged copper)

  • adorable tiny TEC
  • there was an aquarium with transceivers inside but they would not let me take a closeup picture

Vapor Cell Diffractive Optical Elements

you’ve seen vapor cells before, but how about vapor cells shaped into diffractive optical elements like a Fresnel lens? (so that the diffraction depends on detuning / small freq change)

They used it to generate discriminator signals for frequency stabilization. Sadly efficiency is very low ~1%, otherwise this could be optically/magnetically tunable active DOE?

  • Krelman2025: Laser offset stabilization with chip-scale atomic diffractive elements

Ultra-High-Q Niobium Cavities

what do you mean a quality factor of 3e10?

what do you mean “the cavity reached a quality factor of 1e10 at 1 MV/m with a very good reproducibility”??

folks quantum computing is solved, we just need big ass cavities.

(there are smaller cavities with almost comparable Q at power level relevant to quantum stuff)

  • Future Circular Collider Feasibility Study Report Volume 2: Accelerators, technical infrastructure and safety
  • Smaller high Q cavity at single photon level: Niobium coaxial cavities with internal quality factors exceeding 1.5 billion for circuit quantum electrodynamics
  • see also a recent (2 years ago) review on high Q cavities for quantum

Quantum Connector Hardware from D-Wave

cursed connectors from D-wave

  • D-Wave “Supremacy” Controversy Overshadows Real Progress - The company’s quantum machine can solve certain problems blazingly fast
  • when you have a few thousands of qubits: The Most Connected Quantum Computer Built for Business and Research

Sub-Centimeter Magnetically Powered Drone

propeller toy but it’s sub-cm, got a magnet on it, and powered by an alternating magnetic field of a few hundred Hz and ~20 mT

(of course they don’t show you the big coils in the main text)

  • Sui2025: Untethered subcentimeter flying robots

On-Chip Polarization Control and Beam Shaping

stuff like this always makes me wonder, do you really win over free-space optics between the hustle of aligning fiber vs optics, and how much spatial density of control dof you lose moving from SLM/DMD to rings and MZIs. Seems to only make sense if you want high-speed.

(on-chip polarization and beam shape control)

  • Zhou2025: Adaptive reconfiguration and full control of light structures in optical fibers with programmable silicon photonics
  • Chip made by imec and Applied Nano Tools
  • This company looks cool, but holding the fiber array

Size vs Time Drift in Atomic Clocks

Time error vs size for different clocks

  • Marlow2021: A Review of Commercial and Emerging Atomic Frequency Standards

Human Perception of AI Voice Clones

the “Turing test” for voice is kind of done, tested with ElevenLabs over 200+ speakers.

  • Barrington2025: People are poorly equipped to detect AI-powered voice clones
  • Time to learn more accents?

Miniature Ion Trap Frequency Standards

TIL passively pumped ion trap package can be as small as 3 cc, and it can hold vacuum > 10 years & the trap even gets better, with freq stability ~1e-14.

  • Left: Schwindt2015: Miniature trapped-ion frequency standard with 171Yb+ (also)
  • Right:
  • Ticking toward an advanced atomic clock
  • Performance of 3 cm3 ion trap vacuum package sealed for 10 years
  • see also

High-Speed PZT-on-Insulator Electro-Optics

interesting PZT on insulator wafer from OMedaSemi

they claim its EO coefficient is only a bit smaller than BTO, but the modulation bandwidth is much higher

  • OMedaSemi - PZTOI Wafer

Projects

Procrastinating:

  • Droplet walker
  • TabTreeTracker
  • Low cost robot arm
  • Joystick doomscrolling

Planning:

  • Wave demo with ~ 300 Hz water surface wave + 3D printed components

Ongoing:

  • PIC pytorch
    • week 2 (41): [redacted]
    • week 1 (40): built a few simple components. Tested simple backprop and training.
  • WIMS (where is my shit):
    • week 3 (41): got a usb type-C power monitor. Sleep power too high, need debug
    • week 2 (40): Started wearing the camera at home & send thru wifi to desktop. Need to somehow save or send the photo to my phone when outside.
    • week 1 (39): I finally connected to the camera. Quality bad. Ordered new camera (OV5640) from aliexpress.
  • Quantum frequency conversion plot
    • week 4 (41): procrastinating
    • week 3 (40): procrastinating
    • Week 2 (39): published it, currently has piezo-optomechanical approaches
    • Week 1 (38): Gave a talk at PsiQuantum. I like the hat.
  • interactive wave
    • week 1 (37): made a python standalone library, see BPM.
  • fiber2chip blog
  • backyard landscaping:
    • week 15 (41): continue cinder block wall, added geogrid and two more layers of blocks. Also tear down stair form OSB
    • week 14 (40): no progress this week
    • week 13 (39): finished stairs. Started wall with cinder block. Formed and poured concrete at the turn
    • week 12 (38): traveled to LA this weekend! No backyard work.
    • week 11 (37): working on the form of the stairs, deck joists
    • week 10 (36): did not work on it
    • week 9 (35): finished the deck joists and boarded the side of the deck
    • week 8 (34): finished jacuzzi base & deck frame
    • week 7 (33): working on base for Jacuzzi
    • week 6 (32): new year
    • week 5 (31): flattened the bottom level, put down form for concrete curb. Put down linear / grass barrier, and then gravel
    • week 4 (30): Finished the bottom retaining wall
    • week 3 (29): concrete poured for retaining wall beams and deck posts, started putting on retaining wall panels
    • week 2 (28): dig holes, posts inside holes, worked on Living room
    • week 1 (27): first week

Finished:

  • (week 32:) SiPho writeup for avi’s zine.
    • week 36: published on blog
  • Continuing [redacted]
    • Week 36: This has been finished.

Gave up

  • Traveling wave excitation of mechanical ring resonator (see this thread)
  • Tiny Tapeout
    • abort? (because of efabless no funding)
